message.content:
<deliberation>
Step-by-step plan:
1) Read each main node (REG-038, REG-001, REG-008) and note any explicit cross-references.
2) Pull the referenced nodes’ texts where explicitly cited (Art 8(1) → Article 9 paras 1–10). For Art 9(6), include testing-related paras 9(7)–9(10) to provide contextual completeness even though not strictly required.
3) Scan the policy chunk for any statements that directly contradict, narrow, or expand upon the regulatory constraints; treat all omissions as Non-Deviation per rule 4.
4) Identify provable deviations with exact quotes from both the regulation and the policy (rule 4.1), and classify them per the taxonomy.
5) For each main node, include considered references and provide a concise analysis.

Findings per node:
- REG-038 (Art15(1)): Regulation scope is “High-risk AI systems.” Policy states “Applies to: All AI systems.” This is broader than required (over-compliance). No other contradictions; omissions (accuracy, robustness, cybersecurity, lifecycle consistency) are treated as Non-Deviation.
- REG-001 (Art8(1)): Explicitly references Article 9 risk management system (paras 1–10). Policy again applies to “All AI systems,” which is broader in scope (over-compliance). The policy mentions Impact Assessment and documenting intended use, which aligns conceptually with intended purpose, but lacks explicit linkage to state of the art or the Article 9 risk management system; treat as omissions → Non-Deviation.
- REG-008 (Art9(6)): Testing requirement to ensure consistent performance and compliance. Context from 9(7)–9(10) elaborates real-world testing and metrics/thresholds. Policy mentions documenting limitations “based on the training and testing data that will be used,” but does not contradict testing requirements; omissions → Non-Deviation. The only provable difference is again scope (policy applies to all AI systems), which is over-compliant relative to the regulation that binds high-risk systems.

Therefore, for each of the three nodes, record a single Severity deviation (over-compliance on scope), with precise quotes from the regulation (“High-risk AI systems shall …”) and from the policy (“Applies to: All AI systems.”).
</deliberation>
```json
[
  {
    "reg_id": "REG-038",
    "clause": "Art15(1)",
    "considered_references": [],
    "deviation_found": true,
    "deviations": [
      {
        "deviation_type": "Severity deviation",
        "gdpr_quote": "High-risk AI systems shall be designed and developed in such a way that they achieve an appropriate level of accuracy, robustness, and cybersecurity, and that they perform consistently in those respects throughout their lifecycle.",
        "policy_quote": "Applies to: All AI systems.",
        "mismatch": "The policy applies the requirement to all AI systems, while the regulation addresses high-risk AI systems specifically.",
        "analysis": "The regulation limits the obligation to high-risk systems; the policy broadens scope to all AI systems, which is over-compliant in terms of applicability. No other contradictions identified; missing elements (accuracy/robustness/cybersecurity specifics) are treated as omissions and thus Non-Deviation."
      }
    ]
  },
  {
    "reg_id": "REG-001",
    "clause": "Art8(1)",
    "considered_references": [
      "REG-003",
      "REG-004",
      "REG-005",
      "REG-006",
      "REG-007",
      "REG-009",
      "REG-010",
      "REG-011",
      "REG-012"
    ],
    "deviation_found": true,
    "deviations": [
      {
        "deviation_type": "Severity deviation",
        "gdpr_quote": "High-risk AI systems shall comply with the requirements laid down in this Section, taking into account their intended purpose as well as the generally acknowledged state of the art on AI and AI-related technologies. The risk management system referred to in Article 9 shall be taken into account when ensuring compliance with those requirements.",
        "policy_quote": "Applies to: All AI systems.",
        "mismatch": "The policy extends applicability beyond high-risk AI systems, whereas the regulation targets high-risk AI systems.",
        "analysis": "Art8(1) applies to high-risk systems and integrates Article 9’s risk management system (REG-003 to REG-012). The policy broadened scope to all AI systems, which is stricter than required (over-compliance). Other elements (state of the art, explicit risk management steps) are not contradicted but simply not present in this chunk; per rules, omissions are Non-Deviation."
      }
    ]
  },
  {
    "reg_id": "REG-008",
    "clause": "Art9(6)",
    "considered_references": [
      "REG-009",
      "REG-010"
    ],
    "deviation_found": true,
    "deviations": [
      {
        "deviation_type": "Severity deviation",
        "gdpr_quote": "High-risk AI systems shall be tested for the purpose of identifying the most appropriate and targeted risk management measures. Testing shall ensure that high-risk AI systems perform consistently for their intended purpose and that they are in compliance with the requirements set out in this Section.",
        "policy_quote": "Applies to: All AI systems.",
        "mismatch": "The policy’s applicability is broader than the regulation’s, which requires testing duties specifically for high-risk AI systems.",
        "analysis": "Art9(6) testing duties target high-risk systems, further elaborated by Art9(7)–(10) (e.g., real-world testing, prior-defined metrics). The policy extends scope to all AI systems—over-compliance. No conflicting statements about testing methods or metrics; the remainder are omissions and thus Non-Deviation."
      }
    ]
  }
]
```
